schengen_info.description: France is a founding member of the Schengen Area, which allows free movement across 29 European countries without border checks. As a Schengen member, France shares a common visa policy with other Schengen states, meaning a single Schengen visa grants access to all member countries.

visa_free.description: Citizens of the following countries can enter France without a visa for stays up to 90 days within any 180-day period for tourism, business, or family visits

etias.description: Starting mid-2026, visa-exempt travelers will need ETIAS authorization

application.where: French consulate or TLScontact/VFS Global visa center

faq.question: Do I need a visa to visit France?
faq.answer: Citizens of the EU/EEA, USA, Canada, UK, Australia, Japan, and many other countries can visit France visa-free for up to 90 days. Check the France-Visas portal for your nationality.

faq.question: What is the Schengen 90-day rule?
faq.answer: You can stay in the Schengen Area (including France) for a maximum of 90 days within any 180-day rolling period. Days spent in any Schengen country count towards this limit.

faq.question: What is ETIAS?
faq.answer: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) is a new travel authorization required for visa-exempt travelers visiting the Schengen Area, expected to launch mid-2026. It costs EUR 7 and is valid for 3 years.

faq.question: Can I work in France on a tourist visa?
faq.answer: No. Tourist visas (Type C) do not permit employment. You need a Type D long-stay visa with work authorization, or a Working Holiday Visa if your country is eligible.
